```json
{
  "module": "ui-design-system",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 62,
    "domain_depth": 45,
    "data_richness": 40,
    "interaction_quality": 55,
    "ai_integration": 10,
    "mobile_first": 35,
    "performance": 58,
    "typescript_quality": 60,
    "error_handling": 38,
    "realtime": 20,
    "accessibility": 42,
    "security": 50,
    "differentiation": 40,
    "enterprise_readiness": 45
  },
  "overall_score": 43,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "ContextMenu missing ARIA roles and keyboard trap handling on menu items",
      "file": "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx",
      "description": "ContextMenu items lack role='menuitem', tabIndex, and proper focus management. The menu div captures ArrowUp/Down but individual items are not focusable buttons with menuitem role consistently applied. Screen readers cannot navigate the menu. This violates Global Acceptance Standards requiring zero critical axe-core violations and full keyboard navigability.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ContextMenu component: ensure every clickable item rendered inside the menu has role='menuitem' and tabIndex={-1} on the button element. BEFORE: menu items may render without explicit role='menuitem' and tabIndex attributes, meaning keyboard navigation via ArrowUp/ArrowDown calls focus() on elements that are not focusable. AFTER: each menu item button element has role='menuitem', tabIndex={-1}, and aria-label set to the item label. The menu container div already has role='menu'. Also add aria-orientation='vertical' to the menu container. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "No global error boundary or toast notification system for API failures",
      "file": "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx",
      "description": "The UI design system module provides no ErrorBoundary wrapper, no toast notification component, and no global error handling pattern. The Global Acceptance Standards require 'Network failures: toast notification + inline retry button. Never a full-page error for a single failed fetch.' and 'Optimistic update rollbacks: toast with message.' Without a toast system, every page fails this criterion. This is the highest-impact missing piece across the entire app.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, at the top of the file after imports, add a new exported component called Toast and a ToastProvider context. BEFORE: No toast notification component exists anywhere in the UI design system. Pages have no way to show transient success/error/warning messages. AFTER: Export a Toast component that renders a fixed-position container (bottom-right, z-index 9999) displaying messages with severity variants (success: green, error: red, warning: amber, info: blue) matching the design standards color tokens. Each toast auto-dismisses after 5 seconds with a close button. Export a useToast() hook that returns { showToast: (message: string, severity: 'success'|'error'|'warning'|'info') => void }. Use React context with useState to manage a toast queue (max 5 visible). Include role='alert' and aria-live='assertive' on error toasts, aria-live='polite' on others. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Breadcrumbs component has no responsive behavior and overflows on mobile",
      "file": "src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx",
      "description": "FolderBreadcrumbs uses flexWrap but has no max-width constraint, no ellipsis on long paths, and no collapsing behavior for deep folder hierarchies on mobile. At 375px width (iPhone SE, required by Global Standards), a 4-level deep folder path will overflow. Touch targets for crumb buttons are ~28px tall, below the required 44px minimum.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the FolderBreadcrumbs component and the crumbButtonStyle constant: BEFORE: crumbButtonStyle has padding of spacing['1'] (4px) vertical making buttons ~28px tall. The nav container has no overflow handling for mobile. Long folder names truncate per-item but the overall breadcrumb bar can overflow the viewport. AFTER: Change crumbButtonStyle padding to `${spacing['2']} ${spacing['3']}` to achieve minimum 44px touch target height. Add a media query approach using a wrapper div: on screens below 768px, show only the last 2 breadcrumb segments with an ellipsis button '...' that expands the full path on tap. Add overflow: 'hidden' and maxWidth: '100%' to the nav element. Ensure the nav has a horizontal scroll fallback with style overflowX: 'auto' and WebkitOverflowScrolling: 'touch' for mobile. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "visual_polish",
      "title": "No skeleton loader component exists in the design system",
      "file": "src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx",
      "description": "Global Acceptance Standards mandate 'skeleton loaders MUST match the final layout geometry' and 'Use skeleton screens, not spinners.' The design system provides no reusable Skeleton component. Every page is forced to either implement its own or show nothing during loading, violating the 'never a blank screen' requirement. This is referenced in the design standards as requiring pulse animation (opacity 0.3 to 0.7, 1.5s cycle).",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, after the existing exports at the end of the file, add a new exported Skeleton component. BEFORE: No Skeleton component exists in the design system. Pages have no standardized loading placeholder. AFTER: Export a Skeleton component with props: width (string|number, default '100%'), height (string|number, default 20), borderRadius (string|number, default 8), variant ('text'|'card'|'circle'|'rectangle', default 'rectangle'). The component renders a div with backgroundColor '#E5E7EB', the specified dimensions, and a CSS keyframe animation called 'skeleton-pulse' that oscillates opacity from 0.3 to 0.7 over 1.5s infinite. For variant='circle', force borderRadius to '50%'. For variant='card', set default height to 120 and borderRadius to 12. For variant='text', set default height to 14. Inject the keyframe via a style tag with a deduplication check. Also export a SkeletonGroup component that accepts count (number) and gap (number, default 8) and renders that many Skeleton children in a flex column. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C1-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "ContextMenu has no animation on open/close and no divider rendering",
      "file": "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx",
      "description": "The ContextMenu component checks for item.divider in the type definition but the provided code snippet shows no divider rendering logic. The menu appears instantly with no fade/scale transition, violating the design standard requiring 'Modal: Fade + scale from 95% to 100% in 150ms'. The menu also has no shadow matching the specified 'elevated' shadow value from the design standards.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ContextMenu component where the menu div is rendered (the div with role='menu'): BEFORE: The menu appears and disappears instantly with no transition. Items with divider=true are not visually separated. The menu box-shadow may not match the design standard elevated shadow. AFTER: Add an inline style for the menu container: boxShadow set to '0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)' (the elevated shadow from design standards), transform and opacity transition. On mount, start with opacity 0 and transform 'scale(0.95)', then immediately set to opacity 1 and transform 'scale(1)' using a useEffect with requestAnimationFrame. Set transition to 'opacity 150ms ease-out, transform 150ms ease-out'. For items where divider is true, render a 1px solid #E5E7EB horizontal rule (div with height 1px, background #E5E7EB, margin 4px 0) before the item button. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore ships a mature component library with consistent loading states, toast notifications, and mobile-optimized navigation. Fieldwire's mobile-first design system includes swipe gesture primitives and offline-aware UI components. SiteSync's design system lacks foundational primitives (skeleton loaders, toasts, error boundaries) that competitors take for granted, making it impossible for any page to meet the Global Acceptance Standards without each page reinventing these patterns.",
  "summary": "The UI Design System module is severely underpowered for an enterprise construction PM app. It provides only two components (Breadcrumbs and ContextMenu), both with accessibility gaps and missing mobile responsiveness. Critical foundational components are entirely absent: no skeleton loader, no toast notification system, no error boundary, no empty state template, no form input primitives, no status badge component, and no metric card component. The Global Acceptance Standards require skeleton loaders on every page, toast notifications for all mutations, and 44px touch targets — none of which can be met with the current system. The ContextMenu lacks proper ARIA attributes and animated transitions. The Breadcrumbs overflow on mobile. Scores are low across all dimensions because this module is the foundation that every other module depends on, and it provides almost nothing. The five fix prompts target the highest-impact gaps: accessibility on the existing ContextMenu, a toast system for error handling, mobile responsiveness on Breadcrumbs, a skeleton loader primitive, and interaction polish on the ContextMenu."
}
```
